function.name: open
function.description: Read a file and display it in a scrollable editor window (100 lines at a time). Output format: line numbers (`N | content`), total line count, and visible range. You MUST open a file before you can edit, insert, scroll, or search within it.

line_number.description: Center the view on this line.

function.name: goto
function.description: Move the editor window to show the specified line number. A file must already be open.

line_number.description: The 1-based line number to jump to.

function.name: scroll_up
function.description: Scroll the editor window up by one page (100 lines). A file must already be open.

function.name: scroll_down
function.description: Scroll the editor window down by one page (100 lines). A file must already be open.

function.name: create
function.description: Create a new empty file and open it in the editor. Parent directories are created automatically.

filename.description: Relative path for the new file.

function.name: edit
function.description: Replace text in the currently open file. You MUST open the file first. Replaces the first occurrence of `search` with `replace`. Set `replace-all` to true for all occurrences. The `search` string must exactly match existing content (including whitespace and indentation). Returns an error if `search` is not found — double-check with `open` or `search_file` first.

search.description: The exact text to find in the current file.

replace-all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of just the first. Default: false.

function.name: insert
function.description: Insert text into the currently open file. Appends at the end by default, or after the specified line.

text.description: The text to insert (can be multiple lines with \n).

line.description: Insert after this line number (1-based). Omit to append at end.

function.name: find_file
function.description: Search for files by name pattern using glob syntax (*, ?). Returns up to 50 matching file paths.

file_name.description: Glob pattern to match against file names (e.g., "*.rs", "config.*").

dir.description: Subdirectory to search in. Default: workspace root.

function.name: search_dir
function.description: Search for a text string across all files in a directory. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Up to 50 results.

search_term.description: The text to search for (exact substring match).

dir.description: Subdirectory to search in. Default: workspace root.

function.name: search_file
function.description: Search for a text string within a specific file or the currently open file. Returns matching lines with line numbers.

file.description: File to search. Default: currently open file.

function.name: filemap
function.description: Show the structure of a source file: function/class/method signatures with line numbers, bodies collapsed. Useful for understanding a file without reading every line. Works with Python, Rust, JavaScript, TypeScript, Go, and Java.

function.name: submit
function.description: Mark the current task as complete. Call this when you have finished all requested changes.

function.name: allow_outside_workspace
function.description: Enable or disable access to files outside the workspace directory. By default, all file operations are restricted to the workspace root. Call this tool with allow=true if the user asks you to access absolute paths or files outside the workspace.

allow.description: true to enable, false to disable. Default: true.
